info.title: Google Gemini Generate Content API
info.description: Google Gemini API for content generation (text, image, audio) based on official documentation.

post.summary: Generate content using the specified Gemini model

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Which Gemini model to run, given in the path as `models/{model}`.

post.description: Invoke a Gemini model to generate content from a multi-turn conversation. Drive the output with tools, safety settings, a system instruction, and generation parameters, and optionally reuse previously cached context.

contents.description: The ordered conversation turns sent to the model; each turn carries its content parts and the role that produced them. This is the primary input the model responds to.

tools.description: Tools the model is allowed to call during generation, enabling capabilities such as function calling.

toolConfig.description: Configuration that governs how the model selects and invokes the provided tools.

safetySettings.description: Per-request safety controls that adjust how generated content is filtered against harm categories.

systemInstruction.description: A top-level instruction that sets the model's persona, rules, or task framing, applied across the whole conversation.

generationConfig.description: Parameters that shape the generation itself, such as sampling and output-length controls.

cachedContent.description:
Reference to previously cached context to reuse, avoiding resending large shared content on each call.
[⚠️Note:源码未声明该缓存 ID 的获取接口与生效条件,待研发确认。]

text.description: The text payload of a part, used when the part carries written content.

parts.description: The pieces that make up one conversation turn. A part is either a text segment or inline binary data (such as an image), letting a single turn mix modalities.

role.description: Who authored this turn, distinguishing system guidance, end-user input, and prior model output so the model reads the dialogue in the right voice.

candidates.description: The content the model generated; each candidate is one self-contained response made up of content parts and its role.

code.description: Numeric error code identifying the failure category.

message.description: Human-readable explanation of what went wrong.

status.description: Symbolic status string for the error, suitable for programmatic branching.

error.description: The error payload returned when generation fails, carrying a code, a human-readable message, and a symbolic status.
